title: Dead Lettering, Poison Messages, and Parking · Execution Trace: Parking Remediation Workflow
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must interpret x-death evidence defensively, prevent cycles, retain bounded context, and authorize replay through a separate path; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the parking remediation workflow increment in a Go poison-message remediation workflow. Learners…
objectives: Give parked messages bounded retention, searchable redacted evidence, owner, repair, replay authorization, duplicate safety, and closure criteria; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Account for RabbitMQ 4.x quorum delivery limit defaults, x-delivery-count, policy changes, group requeues, and intentional dead-letter retention; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
